behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS and MOCK-HIDE are both true: a unit test over summary-builder logic can pass while real persisted findings for that round still contain FAIL. The failure boundary is the gate check across summary write path + findings store query scoped by round.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real test DB/repository layer. Seed one review round with per-dimension findings including at least one FAIL, then attempt to persist a PASS summary for that same round through the normal service path. Assert the write is rejected (or transaction rolled back) and an error/result code indicates conflicting stored FAIL findings.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS is true if you only test a pure verdict function with mocked inputs; the real defect came from trusting summary text instead of persisted findings. Minimum boundary is wiring between gate computation and findings persistence/read path.
behaviors.test_description: With real local persistence, create a round where stored per-dimension findings include FAIL, but summary text says all PASS. Run stop-gate computation via the production entrypoint. Assert verdict is non-PASS and that the decision rationale references stored findings rather than summary text.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: A unit test can verify flag checks, but it can miss real audit-path failures (warning not persisted, wrong actor/round metadata, or bypass via alternate write path). Need modules wired together: override handling + summary storage + audit sink.
behaviors.test_description: Run through the real summary update API/service with and without `override=true`. Without the flag, assert override attempt is rejected. With the flag, assert update is accepted and a warning audit record is persisted containing round id, actor, timestamp, and override reason.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the review loop cannot finalize PASS unless a fresh review-agent round is executed on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is stale-round reuse after code changes. This can be caught without real LLM behavior by validating orchestration state: diff/version fingerprint must match a newly executed round before PASS. Agentic quality is not the assertion here.
behaviors.test_description: Create a scenario with an earlier review round, then apply a simulated Phase 4 fix that changes the diff fingerprint. Attempt finalization without creating a new review round: assert PASS is blocked. Then execute a new round record tied to the updated diff and assert finalization can proceed (subject to findings).

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings, the flow emits an escalation outcome instead of PASS.
behaviors.justification: This is a state-machine/persistence boundary across rounds and findings severity. Unit tests can miss counter resets, round accounting, or query filters. No real OS/network/LLM dependency is required to catch the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Seed max-allowed rounds in the real local store, with at least one unresolved MUST-FIX finding in the latest round. Invoke completion decision logic via the normal flow entrypoint. Assert outcome is `ESCALATE` (or equivalent), PASS is disallowed, and escalation metadata includes unresolved MUST-FIX references.
